pageTitle: Feast of St. Andrew, Apostle - November 30, 2024 - Liturgical Calendar | Catholic Culture

celebrations.celebration: Feast of St. Andrew

It is truly right and just, our duty and our salvation, always and everywhere to give you thanks, Lord, holy Father, almighty and eternal God.

It is truly right and just, our duty and our salvation, always and everywhere to give you thanks, Lord, holy Father, almighty and eternal God, through Christ our Lord.

Dear brethren, since the beginning of Lent until now we have prepared our hearts by penance and charitable works. Today we gather together to herald with the whole Church the beginning of the celebration of our Lord’s Paschal Mystery, that is to say, of his Passion and Resurrection. For it was to accomplish this mystery that he entered his own city of Jerusalem. Therefore, with all faith and devotion, let us commemorate the Lord’s entry into the city for our salvation, following in his footsteps, so that, being made by his grace partakers of the Cross, we may have a share also in his Resurrection and in his life.

pageTitle: Feast of St. Mary Magdalene - July 22, 2025 - Liturgical Calendar | Catholic Culture

celebrations.celebration: Feast of St. Mary Magdalene

propers.text: The Lord said to Mary Magdalene: Go to my brothers and tell them: I am going to my Father and your Father, to my God and your God.
Tell us Mary, what did you see on the way? I saw the glory of the risen Christ, I saw his empty tomb.

Jesus said to her, "Woman, why are you weeping? Whom are you looking for?" She thought it was the gardener and said to him, "Sir, if you carried him away, tell me where you laid him, and I will take him." Jesus said to her, "Mary!" She turned and said to him in Hebrew, "Rabbouni," which means Teacher. Jesus said to her, “Stop holding on to me, for I have not yet ascended to the Father. But go to my brothers and tell them, 'I am going to my Father and your Father, to my God and your God.'" Mary Magdalene went and announced to the disciples, "I have seen the Lord," and then reported what he told her.
It is truly right and just, our duty and our salvation, to glorify you in all things, almighty Father, whose mercy is not less than your power, through Christ our Lord.
The love of Christ impels us, so that those who live may live no longer for themselves, but for him who died for them and was raised.

Since the Passover of the Jews was near, Jesus went up to Jerusalem. He found in the temple area those who sold oxen, sheep, and doves, as well as the money-changers seated there. He made a whip out of cords and drove them all out of the temple area, with the sheep and oxen, and spilled the coins of the money-changers and overturned their tables, and to those who sold doves he said, “Take these out of here, and stop making my Father’s house a marketplace.” His disciples recalled the words of Scripture, Zeal for your house will consume me. At this the Jews answered and said to him, “What sign can you show us for doing this?” Jesus answered and said to them, “Destroy this temple and in three days I will raise it up.”

Jesus, full of the Holy Spirit, returned from the Jordan, and was led by the Spirit for forty days in the wilderness, tempted by the devil. And he ate nothing in those days; and when they were ended, he was hungry. The devil said to him, "If you are the Son of God, command this stone to become bread." And Jesus answered him, "It is written, ‘ Man shall not live by bread alone.’" And the devil took him up. and showed him all the kingdoms of the world in a moment of time, and said to him, "To you I will give all this authority and their glory; for it has been delivered to me, and I give it to whom I will. If you, then, will worship me, it shall all be yours." And Jesus answered him, "It is written, ‘ You shall worship the Lord your God, and him only shall you serve.’ "

Dear brethren, since the beginning of Lent until now we have prepared our hearts by penance and charitable works. Today we gather together to herald with the whole Church the beginning of the celebration of our Lord’s Paschal Mystery, that is to say, of his Passion and Resurrection. For it was to accomplish this mystery that he entered his own city of Jerusalem. Therefore, with all faith and devotion, let us commemorate the Lord’s entry into the city for our salvation, following in his footsteps, so that, being made by his grace partakers of the Cross, we may have a share also in his Resurrection and in his life.

pageTitle: Feast of St. Mary Magdalene - July 22, 2026 - Liturgical Calendar | Catholic Culture

celebrations.celebration: Feast of St. Mary Magdalene

propers.text: The Lord said to Mary Magdalene: Go to my brothers and tell them: I am going to my Father and your Father, to my God and your God.
Tell us Mary, what did you see on the way? I saw the glory of the risen Christ, I saw his empty tomb.

Jesus said to her, "Woman, why are you weeping? Whom are you looking for?" She thought it was the gardener and said to him, "Sir, if you carried him away, tell me where you laid him, and I will take him." Jesus said to her, "Mary!" She turned and said to him in Hebrew, "Rabbouni," which means Teacher. Jesus said to her, “Stop holding on to me, for I have not yet ascended to the Father. But go to my brothers and tell them, 'I am going to my Father and your Father, to my God and your God.'" Mary Magdalene went and announced to the disciples, "I have seen the Lord," and then reported what he told her.
It is truly right and just, our duty and our salvation, to glorify you in all things, almighty Father, whose mercy is not less than your power, through Christ our Lord.
The love of Christ impels us, so that those who live may live no longer for themselves, but for him who died for them and was raised.

As the Father loves me, so I also love you; remain in my love, says the Lord.

pageTitle: Memorial of St. Pius X, Pope - August 21, 2026 - Liturgical Calendar | Catholic Culture

celebrations.celebration: Memorial for St. Pius X

propers.text: The Lord chose him for himself as high priest, and, opening his treasure house, made him rich in all good things.
The word of God is living and effective, able to discern the reflections and thoughts of the heart.
The Good Shepherd has laid down his life for his sheep.

pageTitle: Memorial of the Queenship of the Blessed Virgin Mary - August 22, 2026 - Liturgical Calendar | Catholic Culture

celebrations.celebration: Memorial of the Queenship of Mary

propers.text: At your right hand stands the queen in robes of gold, finely arrayed.
If today you hear his voice, harden not your hearts.

propers.reference: Preface I of the Blessed Virgin Mary
propers.text: It is truly right and just, our duty and our salvation, always and everywhere to give you thanks, Lord, holy Father, almighty and eternal God, and to praise, bless, and glorify your name on the feast day of the Blessed Virgin Mary.
Blessed are you who have believed that what was spoken to you by the Lord will be fulfilled.

pageTitle: Twenty-First Sunday in Ordinary Time - August 23, 2026 - Liturgical Calendar | Catholic Culture

celebrations.celebration: Twenty-First Sunday in Ordinary Time

propers.text: Turn your ear, O Lord, and answer me; save the servant who trust in you, my God. Have mercy on me, O Lord, for I cry to you all the day long.
You are Peter and upon this rock I will build my Church and the gates of the netherworld shall not prevail against it.
The earth is replete with the fruits of your work, O Lord; you bring forth bread from the earth and wine to cheer the heart.

pageTitle: Feast of St. Bartholomew, Apostle - August 24, 2026 - Liturgical Calendar | Catholic Culture

celebrations.celebration: Feast of St. Bartholomew

propers.text: Proclaim the salvation of God day by day; tell among the nations his glory.
Rabbi, you are the Son of God; you are the King of Israel.
It is truly right and just, our duty and our salvation, always and everywhere to give you thanks, Lord, holy Father, almighty and eternal God.
I confer a kingdom on you, just as my Father has conferred one on me, that you may eat and drink at my table in my kingdom, says the Lord.

pageTitle: Tuesday of the Twenty-First Week in Ordinary Time; Opt Mem of St. Louis of France; Opt Mem of St. Joseph Calasanz, Priest - August 25, 2026 - Liturgical Calendar | Catholic Culture

celebrations.celebration: Tuesday of the Twenty-First Week in Ordinary Time

propers.text: Turn your ear, O Lord, and answer me; save the servant who trust in you, my God. Have mercy on me, O Lord, for I cry to you all the day long.
The word of God is living and effective, able to discern reflections and thoughts of the heart.
The earth is replete with the fruits of your work, O Lord; you bring forth bread from the earth and wine to cheer the heart.
Whoever eats my flesh and drinks my blood has eternal life, says the Lord, and I will raise him up on the last day.

celebrations.celebration: Optional Memorial of St. Louis of France

propers.text: The law of truth was in his mouth; no dishonesty was found on his lips. He walked with me in integrity and peace, and turned many away from evil.
I give you a new commandment: love one another as I have loved you.
Blessed are the clean of heart, for they shall see God. Blessed are the peacemakers, for they shall be called children of God.

celebrations.celebration: Optional Memorial of St. Joseph Calasanz

propers.text: Whoever obeys and teaches the commandments of the Lord will be called great in the Kingdom of Heaven, says the Lord.
Remain in my love, says the Lord; whoever remains in me and I in him will bear much fruit.
Whoever follows me will not walk in darkness, but will have the light of life, says the Lord.

pageTitle: Wednesday of the Twenty-First Week in Ordinary Time - August 26, 2026 - Liturgical Calendar | Catholic Culture

celebrations.celebration: Wednesday of the Twenty-First Week in Ordinary Time

propers.text: Turn your ear, O Lord, and answer me; save the servant who trust in you, my God. Have mercy on me, O Lord, for I cry to you all the day long.
Whoever keeps the word o Christ, the love of God is truly perfected in him.
The earth is replete with the fruits of your work, O Lord; you bring forth bread from the earth and wine to cheer the heart.

pageTitle: Memorial of St. Monica - August 27, 2026 - Liturgical Calendar | Catholic Culture

celebrations.celebration: Memorial of St. Monica

propers.text: The woman who fears the Lord will herself be praised. Her children have called her most blessed, her husband has sung her praises.
The word of God is living and effective, able to discern reflections and thoughts of the heart.
The Kingdom of Heaven is like a merchant who travels in search of fine pearls and who, on finding one of great price, sold everything and bought it.

pageTitle: Memorial of St. Augustine, Bishop and Doctor of the Church - August 28, 2026 - Liturgical Calendar | Catholic Culture

celebrations.celebration: Memorial of St. Augustine

propers.text: In the midst of the Church he opened his mouth, and the Lord filled him with the spirit of wisdom and understanding and clothed him in a robe of glory.
Whoever keeps the word of Christ, the love of God is truly perfected in him.
Thus says the Lord: You have but one teacher, the Christ, and you are all brothers.

pageTitle: Memorial of the Passion of Saint John the Baptist - August 29, 2026 - Liturgical Calendar | Catholic Culture

celebrations.celebration: Memorial of the Passion of St. John the Baptist

propers.text: I spoke, O Lord, of your decrees before kings, and was not confounded; I pondered your commands and loved them greatly.
Blessed are those who are persecuted for the sake of righteousness, for theirs is the Kingdom of heaven.

propers.reference: Preface, The Mission of the Precursor
propers.text: It is truly right and just, our duty and our salvation, always and everywhere to give you thanks, Lord, holy Father, almighty and eternal God, through Christ our Lord.
John answered and said: He must increase; but I must decrease.

pageTitle: Twenty-Second Sunday in Ordinary Time - August 30, 2026 - Liturgical Calendar | Catholic Culture

celebrations.celebration: Twenty-Second Sunday in Ordinary Time

propers.text: Have mercy on me, O Lord, for I cry all the day long. O Lord, you are good and forgiving, full of mercy to all who call to you.
May the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ enlighten the eyes of our hearts, that we may know what is the hope that belongs to our call.
How great is the goodness, Lord, that you keep for those who fear you.
Blessed are the peacemakers, for they shall be called children of God. Blessed are they who are persecuted for the sake of righteousness, for theirs is the Kingdom of Heaven.

pageTitle: Monday of the Twenty-Second Week in Ordinary Time - August 31, 2026 - Liturgical Calendar | Catholic Culture

celebrations.celebration: Monday of the Twenty-Second Week in Ordinary Time

propers.text: Have mercy on me, O Lord, for I cry all the day long. O Lord, you are good and forgiving, full of mercy to all who call to you.
The Spirit of the Lord is upon me; he has sent me to bring glad tidings to the poor.
How great is the goodness, Lord, that you keep for those who fear you.
Blessed are the peacemakers, for they shall be called children of God. Blessed are they who are persecuted for the sake of righteousness, for theirs is the Kingdom of Heaven.

pageTitle: Memorial of St. John of the Cross, Priest and Doctor - December 14, 2024 - Liturgical Calendar | Catholic Culture

celebrations.celebration: Mass Propers of the Memorial of St. John of the Cross

propers.text: May I never boast, except in the Cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, through which the world has been crucified to me, and I to the world.
Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
Whoever wishes to come after me must deny himself, take up his cross, and follow me, says the Lord.

pageTitle: Thursday after Ash Wednesday - March 06, 2025 - Liturgical Calendar | Catholic Culture

celebrations.celebration: Thursday after Ash Wednesday

propers.text: When I cried to the Lord, he heard my voice; he rescued me from those who attack me. Entrust your cares to the Lord, and he will support you.
Repent, says the Lord; the Kingdom of heaven is at hand.
Create a pure heart for me, O God; renew a steadfast spirit within me.

pageTitle: Good Friday of the Lord’s Passion - April 18, 2025 - Liturgical Calendar | Catholic Culture

celebrations.celebration: Propers for Good Friday of the Lord's Passion

propers.reference: Antiphon during the Adoration of the Holy Cross, Cf. Ps 67 (66):2
propers.text: We adore your Cross, O Lord, we praise and glorify your holy Resurrection, for behold, because of the wood of a tree joy has come to the whole world.

Christ became obedient to the point of death, even death on a cross. Because of this, God greatly exalted him and bestowed on him the name which is above every other name.Prayer Over the People: May abundant blessing, O Lord, we pray, descend upon your people, who have honored the Death of your Son in the hope of their resurrection: may pardon come, comfort be given, holy faith increase, and everlasting redemption be made secure. Through Christ our Lord. Amen.

pageTitle: Holy Saturday — Easter Vigil - April 19, 2025 - Liturgical Calendar | Catholic Culture

celebrations.celebration: Propers for the Easter Vigil

propers.text: Alleluia, Alleluia, Alleluia
Springs of water, bless the Lord; praise and exalt him above all for ever.
I saw water flowing from the Temple, from its right-hand side, alleluia; and all to whom this water came were saved and shall say: Alleluia, alleluia.
It is truly right and just, our duty and our salvation, at all times to acclaim you, O Lord, but on this night above all to laud you yet more gloriously, when Christ our Passover has been sacrificed.
Christ our Passover has been sacrificed; therefore let us keep the feast with the unleavened bread of purity and truth, alleluia.

pageTitle: Friday of the First Week in Ordinary Time - January 16, 2026 - Liturgical Calendar | Catholic Culture

celebrations.celebration: Mass Propers of Friday of the First Week in Ordinary Time

propers.text: Upon a lofty throne, I saw a man seated, whom a host of angels adore, singing in unison: Behold him, the name of whose empire is eternal.
A great prophet has arisen in our midst and God has visited his people.
With you, O Lord, is the fountain of life, and in your light we see light.
I have come that they may have life, and have it more abundantly, says the Lord.

pageTitle: Solemnity of the Most Holy Trinity - June 15, 2025 - Liturgical Calendar | Catholic Culture

celebrations.celebration: Solemnity of the Most Holy Trinity

propers.text: Blest be God the Father, and the Only Begotten Son of God, and also the Holy Spirit, for he has shown us his merciful love.
Glory to the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit; to God who is, who was, and who is to come.

propers.reference: Preface: The Mystery of the Most Holy Trinity
propers.text: It is truly right and just, our duty and our salvation, always and everywhere to give you thanks, Lord, holy Father, almighty and eternal God.
Since you are children of God, God has sent into your hearts the Spirit of his Son, the Spirit who cries out: Abba, Father.

pageTitle: Solemnity of the Most Holy Trinity - May 31, 2026 - Liturgical Calendar | Catholic Culture

pageTitle: Thursday of the Ninth Week in Ordinary Time - June 04, 2026 - Liturgical Calendar | Catholic Culture

celebrations.celebration: Thursday of the Ninth Week in Ordinary Time

propers.text: Turn to me and have mercy on me, O Lord, for I am alone and poor. See my lowliness and suffering and take away all my sins, my God.
Our Savior Jesus Christ has destroyed death and brought life to light through the Gospel.
To you I call, for you will surely heed me, O God; turn your ear to me; hear my words.

pageTitle: Memorial of St. Boniface, Bishop and Martyr - June 05, 2026 - Liturgical Calendar | Catholic Culture

celebrations.celebration: Memorial of St. Boniface

propers.text: This holy man fought to the death for the law of his God and did not fear the words of the godless, for he was built on solid rock.
I am the resurrection and the life, says the Lord; whoever believes in me will never die.
Whoever wishes to come after me, must deny himself, take up his cross, and follow me, says the Lord.

pageTitle: Second Sunday of Advent - December 08, 2024 - Liturgical Calendar | Catholic Culture

celebrations.celebration: Second Sunday of Advent

propers.text: O people of Sion, behold, the Lord will come to save the nations, and the Lord will make the glory of his voice heard in the joy of your heart.
Prepare the way of the Lord, make straight his paths: all flesh shall see the salvation of God.
It is truly right and just, our duty and our salvation, always and everywhere to give you thanks, Lord, holy Father, almighty and eternal God, through Christ our Lord.
Jerusalem, arise and stand upon the heights, and behold the joy which comes to you from God.
